fields.description:
Tracks GitHub issue navapbc/oscer#766.

Part of the exclusion/exception determination work under epic #732 (Data Source Hierarchy). Prerequisite for #767 and #768. Depends on #761.

## Background

`ExclusionDeterminationService` evaluates exclusion signals as point-in-time booleans on `Certifications::MemberData`. Some exclusion conditions, such as incarceration, apply only for a specific date range, and whether the member is excluded depends on when those dates fall:

- If the condition is active during the certification month, the member is excluded.
- If the condition is not active during the certification month but its dates fall within the certification lookback, it counts as a mandatory exception rather than an exclusion.

A boolean cannot express this distinction. This introduces date-based representation for those signals, following the pattern used for external exceptions in #765, which stores the signals as arrays of dates on `MemberData` and matches them at month granularity.

## User Story

As a Medicaid program processing a certification, I want exclusion signals stored as dates, so the determination can tell whether a condition is active during the certification month (an exclusion) or is no longer active but fell within the lookback (a mandatory exception).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `Certifications::MemberData` represents a lapsing exclusion signal (incarceration) as an array of dates, following the `dates_*` pattern from #765.
- [ ] `ExclusionDeterminationService` determines the member excluded only when the signal includes the certification month (the month of `certification_requirements.certification_date`), matching the month-level comparison used for exceptions.
- [ ] A signal that does not include the certification month does not produce an exclusion, even when its dates fall within the lookback.
- [ ] Demo tooling can seed the date signal, as in #765.

## Out-of-scope

- Emitting the mandatory exception for a signal that is not active during the certification month but whose dates fall within the lookback. That belongs to the ordered determination flow tracked in #767.
- Sourcing incarceration dates from an external data source, handled under the data-source contract work (#755). This issue populates the signal from `MemberData`.

## Dependencies & Risks

- Depends on #761 (return the highest-priority exclusion). `main`'s `ExclusionDeterminationService` still evaluates the pre-#761 aggregate fact, so this builds on the post-#761 shape.
- Builds on the pattern merged in #765 and #757.

## Links & Resources

- Data Source Hierarchy tech spec, Â§"Exceptions from former exclusions" (linked from epic #732)
- Related: #765, #757 (exception date pattern), #761 (highest-priority exclusion)
fields.title: Represent lapsing exclusion signals as lookback dates in MemberData
